user_message: I want to start accepting BCH in my business. What is the simplest setup?
expected_behavior: recommend a simple wallet plus QR or POS setup
mention backing up the recovery phrase
suggest pricing in local fiat first if appropriate
keep the answer practical and merchant-friendly
bad_answer: You need a full custom crypto stack before you can accept BCH seriously.
must_not_say: Merchant adoption requires a full node on day one
Do not worry about wallet backup at all

user_message: Why would a BCH merchant use an xPub with a POS flow?
expected_behavior: explain that xPub can help generate fresh receiving addresses
connect it to merchant POS flows
keep the answer practical rather than overly technical
bad_answer: An xPub is basically just a private key that merchants can share anywhere.
must_not_say: xPub and seed phrase are basically the same thing
Merchants should post their seed phrase to make receiving easier

user_message: We have more than one owner. Should we use multisig for BCH?
expected_behavior: explain multisig as a business-control and risk-management tool
connect it to shared control and reduced single-point failure
avoid overselling complexity where not needed
bad_answer: No, businesses should always use a single personal wallet for speed.
must_not_say: Shared treasury does not need process
Anyone should be able to move business funds alone

user_message: A merchant asked me whether BCH is legal, how to enter and exit, and what it costs. What should I focus on first?
expected_behavior: cover cost acceptance basic conversion path and practical uses
keep the tone grounded and adoption-focused
bad_answer: Ignore those boring questions and just sell them on the philosophy.
must_not_say: Practical entry and exit do not matter
Merchants should accept first and ask questions later

user_message: What does spend and replace mean in a BCH lifestyle context?
expected_behavior: explain it as using BCH in real commerce while replenishing holdings
connect it to adoption and routine use
avoid treating BCH only as passive speculation
bad_answer: It means never spending BCH under any circumstances.
must_not_say: Using BCH in commerce weakens it
The only valid BCH use is holding forever

user_message: A major P2P exchange shut down. How should a BCH user think about fallback options?
expected_behavior: mention reputation-based local and community alternatives carefully
distinguish volatility hedging from fiat entry-exit needs
mention privacy hygiene like post-purchase separation where relevant
bad_answer: Just use the first random custodial service you find and skip all privacy concerns.
must_not_say: All fallback exchange methods are identical
Reputation and process do not matter in P2P exchange
